center.name: 納亞里特州
center.summary: 納亞里特州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括Black turnstone、Golden-cheeked woodpecker、White-striped woodcreeper等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It winters on rocky shores along the Pacific coast of North America from southern Alaska southwards as far as north-west Mexico where it occurs in Baja California and Sonora with a single record from Nayarit

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of golden-cheeked woodpecker is found in western Mexico between Sinaloa and Nayarit

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: sordidus is found only on the Tres Marías Islands off Nayarit. The species inhabits deciduous and gallery forest, arid to semi-humid scrublands, and plantations in the tropical zone.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: umbrosus is found in northwestern Mexico from Sonora and Chihuahua south to Durango, Nayarit, and Jalisco

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|right|The tufted jay can be found in pine-oak forests within Sierra Madre Occidental.|alt=An epiphyte-covered oak tree surrounded by pine trees The tufted jay is endemic to Mexico and is only found in a limited range within the Sierra Madre Occidental, in an area of roughly . Within this mountain range, they can be found in eastern Sinaloa, western Durango, and northern Nayarit. This species is most often found at elevations of , but has been found as low as and as high as . The tufted jay inhabits forests throughout its range

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Sinaloa crow is found in western Mexico from southern Sonora south to southwestern Nayarit and east into western Durango

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: nelsoni is found in southwestern Mexico, from Nayarit, Jalisco and Colima southward to western Guerrero; and C
